evals.prompt:
Render the research artifact to PDF. The artifact is at /tmp/pdf-test/report.md. Save the output to /tmp/pdf-test/report.pdf.

Contents of /tmp/pdf-test/report.md:
---
**Title**: AI Infrastructure Analysis 2025
**Date**: 2025-06-19
**Scope**: Enterprise GPU cloud providers
**Confidence**: High
---

# AI Infrastructure Analysis 2025

## Executive Summary

Enterprise GPU cloud capacity is expanding faster than demand through H1 2026, creating pricing pressure on hyperscalers.

## Key Findings

- GPU spot prices down 38% year-over-year on H100 equivalents
- Three new entrants with >10K H100 capacity launched in Q1 2025
- Hyperscalers defending margin via proprietary silicon (TPU v5, Trainium2)

## Market Dynamics

Incumbents are differentiating on managed services rather than raw compute pricing.

## Diagram

```mermaid
graph TD
  A[GPU Supply] --> B[Spot Price]
  A --> C[Reserved Price]
  B --> D[Hyperscaler Margin]
  C --> D
```

## Conclusion

Enterprise buyers should negotiate reserved capacity contracts now before the supply glut normalises.
evals.expected_output: The skill runs the dependency check first. If pandoc and a PDF engine are present: extracts the mermaid diagram to /tmp/diagram-report-1.mmd, renders it to PNG, writes /tmp/report-pre.md with the PNG reference replacing the mermaid block, runs pandoc with --toc --toc-depth=3 --pdf-engine, writes /tmp/pdf-test/report.pdf, reports its size and path, and removes temp files. The output PDF is non-empty. If pandoc or every engine is absent: prints an informational install message and exits cleanly with no error.

deterministic_checks.description: Dependency check for pandoc runs before any other work
No urn:mif: finding IDs appear anywhere in the transcript
No f_<dim>_<n> finding IDs leak into any output
Skill either produces a PDF or emits an install message — never silent failure

expectations.description: Dependency check runs as the very first shell command before any phase work
expectations.criteria: The 'command -v pandoc' check appears before any pandoc invocation, diagram extraction, or temp file writes in the transcript

expectations.description: If pandoc is present the skill correctly uses --toc and a --pdf-engine flag
expectations.criteria: If a pandoc call appears in the transcript it includes '--toc' and '--pdf-engine=' with a discovered engine name

expectations.description: If pandoc is absent the skill provides an actionable install message and exits cleanly — no error raised
expectations.criteria: If pandoc is absent, the transcript contains install guidance (e.g., 'brew install pandoc') and no Python/shell traceback or unhandled error

evals.prompt:
Render the report to PDF but pandoc is not installed on this machine. Artifact at /tmp/pdf-test/no-pandoc-report.md.

Contents of /tmp/pdf-test/no-pandoc-report.md:
# Supply Chain Resilience Study

## Summary

Global semiconductor lead times have normalised to pre-2020 levels as of Q4 2024.

## Findings

- Lead times: 12 weeks median (down from 52 weeks peak)
- Inventory buffers remain elevated at Tier-1 suppliers

## Conclusion

Procurement teams should reduce safety-stock targets in H1 2025.
evals.expected_output: The skill runs 'command -v pandoc', detects it is absent, prints a human-readable message explaining the channel is unavailable and providing the install command ('brew install pandoc'), and stops. No PDF is written, no pandoc command is attempted, and the process exits cleanly (no error code, no exception).

deterministic_checks.description: pandoc availability is checked via 'command -v pandoc'
Transcript contains an informational message about pandoc being missing with install guidance
No pandoc invocation (starting with 'pandoc ') is made when pandoc is absent
No PDF file is written when pandoc is absent
No urn:mif: IDs appear in the degraded-path output

expectations.description: Skill exits cleanly — no traceback, no error code — user receives actionable guidance
expectations.criteria: The transcript contains no Python traceback, no 'exit 1' error, and the message reads as informational rather than an error condition

expectations.description: Skill does not attempt any downstream phases (diagram extraction, pre.md write, pandoc run) after the failed dependency check
expectations.criteria: No mention of '/tmp/diagram-', 'mermaid-cli', '/tmp/report-pre.md', or a pandoc command appears after the pandoc-not-installed message

evals.prompt:
Render the following artifact to PDF. The artifact at /tmp/pdf-test/diagram-report.md contains a mermaid diagram that will fail to render (mermaid-cli is not available or the diagram has a syntax error). The skill should degrade gracefully and still produce a PDF from the non-diagram content.

Contents of /tmp/pdf-test/diagram-report.md:
**Title**: Competitive Landscape 2025
**Date**: 2025-06-19
**Scope**: SaaS observability tools
**Confidence**: Medium

# Competitive Landscape 2025

## Overview

Four vendors account for 78% of enterprise observability spend.

## Vendor Comparison

| Vendor | Market Share | YoY Growth |
|--------|-------------|------------|
| DatadogCo | 41% | +12% |
| SplunkCo | 22% | -3% |
| NewRelicCo | 9% | +1% |
| Other | 28% | +5% |

```mermaid
sequenceDiagram
  THIS IS INTENTIONALLY BROKEN SYNTAX %%%
  A -->> B: invalid
```

## Conclusion

The incumbent duopoly faces pressure from next-gen lightweight agents.
evals.expected_output: The skill attempts mermaid-cli rendering on the broken diagram block, detects failure, emits a warning identifying the failed diagram number, leaves the block as raw text in the pre-processed Markdown, and continues. A PDF is still produced from the remaining content. The skill does NOT abort due to the diagram failure.

deterministic_checks.description: A warning about the failed diagram is emitted — not a silent failure
No urn:mif: IDs leak into the output despite diagram failure path

expectations.description: Skill continues to produce a PDF (or informs about pandoc absence) after the diagram failure — it does not abort
expectations.criteria: The transcript shows the skill proceeding past the diagram warning: either a pandoc conversion is attempted or, if pandoc is absent, the standard install message appears — but there is no abort or fatal error due to the diagram failure

expectations.description: The warning message identifies which diagram number failed, matching SKILL.md's 'warn with the number' requirement
expectations.criteria: The warning references a diagram number (e.g., 'diagram 1', '#1') rather than a generic 'diagram failed' message

expectations.description: Raw mermaid block text is preserved in the preprocessed Markdown when rendering fails
expectations.criteria: The transcript shows the mermaid block left as text (or the pre.md written with the raw block) rather than replaced by a broken image reference

evals.prompt:
Render the synthesized artifact to PDF. The artifact at /tmp/pdf-test/synthesis-report.md is the output of report-synthesizer and must be rendered cleanly without any internal corpus identifiers leaking into the output.

Contents of /tmp/pdf-test/synthesis-report.md:
**Title**: Autonomous Vehicle Market Analysis
**Date**: 2025-06-19
**Scope**: L4+ autonomous passenger vehicles, NA & EU markets
**Confidence**: Medium-High

# Autonomous Vehicle Market Analysis

## Executive Summary

L4+ autonomous vehicle deployment is entering the commercial scaling phase in limited geofenced corridors, with three operators reporting unit economics that approach breakeven on a per-mile basis.

## Key Findings

- Waymo is operating at an annualized run-rate of 4M paid rides as of Q1 2025
- Insurance frameworks for AV liability remain unresolved in 34 US states
- EU type-approval regulation (UN-R157 extension) expected to clear by Q3 2026

## Competitive Dynamics

Capital intensity is the primary barrier to new entrants; sensor bill-of-materials has fallen 62% since 2022 but software validation costs dominate.

## Outlook

Commercial deployment will remain geofence-constrained through 2027 absent federal preemption legislation.
evals.expected_output: The skill renders the clean synthesized artifact to PDF (or reports pandoc absence). At no point does any internal corpus reference (urn:mif:/f_<dim>_<n> format) appear in the transcript, the pre-processed Markdown, or the PDF. The artifact itself contains no such references (it is a clean report-synthesizer output), and the skill must not inject them. The Phase 0 resolution step reads the artifact file, not any corpus finding files.

deterministic_checks.description: No urn:mif: corpus identifiers appear anywhere in the transcript
No bare f_<dim>_<n> finding IDs appear in the transcript

deterministic_checks.command: if [ -f /tmp/pdf-test/report-pre.md ]; then grep -cE 'urn:mif:|f_[a-z]+_[0-9]+' /tmp/pdf-test/report-pre.md || true; else echo '0'; fi
deterministic_checks.description: The pre-processed Markdown intermediate file contains no corpus identifiers
Skill does not read from any findings/ corpus directory — only from the artifact file

expectations.description: Phase 0 resolves the artifact path and reads only that file — no corpus or findings files are opened
expectations.criteria: The transcript shows the skill reading /tmp/pdf-test/synthesis-report.md only; no 'findings/', 'corpus/', or '.mif' file paths are opened

expectations.description: Skill correctly extracts title, date, scope, and confidence from the artifact header for the title page
expectations.criteria: If pandoc is present, the transcript shows title page construction referencing 'Autonomous Vehicle Market Analysis', '2025-06-19', 'L4+ autonomous', or 'Medium-High' drawn from the artifact header
